behaviors.description: Reject webhook with mismatched `X-Signature-256` and do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue publisher), not just pure hash math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process request harness is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly requires both HTTP 401 and no forward side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler in-process with a test HTTP request containing an invalid signature over the raw body. Assert status is 401 and queue `enqueue` path is not called / queue storage remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before sending HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears across handler and queue persistence boundary. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local in-process wiring plus real queue module/local DB; no external network/process required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text is about write-before-ack ordering, not OS/network-specific effects.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument queue write completion and response send in an integration test; assert write commit timestamp/order occurs before 200 response is emitted.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires interaction between handler enqueue path and async worker trigger; pure unit of one function can miss await/Promise coupling bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real external subprocess/network needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because the failure is async orchestration inside process, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a downstream processor stub that blocks for a long duration after dequeue; send request and assert HTTP response returns before processor resolves.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even under slow concurrent downstream processing.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency depends on real event-loop scheduling, socket handling, and timing under load; in-process fakes/timers can hide contention. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing/backpressure often passes while real server misses SLA. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock" under concurrent slowdown, an OS/runtime timing boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real HTTP service process, generate concurrent traffic while downstream workers are intentionally slow, and measure end-to-end latency; assert webhook acknowledgments remain <=3s.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not written to persistent queue twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedup correctness is at handler+queue persistence interaction (idempotency check/constraint), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store wiring is sufficient; no external OS/network required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit because behavior requires observing persistent write effects across repeated requests.
behaviors.test_description: Submit same event ID twice through handler with valid signatures; assert first write succeeds and second does not create an additional queued record.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, handler returns 503 and includes `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is mapping queue-capacity error to correct HTTP contract (status + header) across module boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real external network/process required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior does not require real OS/network semantics, only local service-queue integration.
behaviors.test_description: Fill queue to capacity in test setup, send another valid webhook, assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header presence/value.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates queue implementation plus persistence backend behavior under capacity checks; unit fakes can miss real constraint enforcement bugs. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is enough; no external subprocess/network inherently required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because requirement is queue-capacity enforcement, not OS/network-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend with small configured capacity, push events until limit, then push one more and assert explicit rejection/no persisted overflow record.
